item.arguments: Use $fw:run for a requirements doc that needs a reviewed technical plan before implementation. Stop at the plan approval gate unless the user approves work.
success_definition: Starts in shape and uses plan mode.
Emits a compact handoff card with Stage, Artifact, Ready, Open decisions, Evidence, and Next.
Treats reviewed plan approval as a gate before work.
Carries the plan artifact and document-review result forward.

item.arguments: Use $fw:run on an approved plan with two implementation units and TDD proof requirements, then carry it to review when implementation is ready.
success_definition: Starts in work from the approved plan.
Uses a work-ready gate that accounts for implementation, TDD evidence, verification, and unresolved blockers.
Emits a handoff card that names review as next and carries evidence forward.
Does not jump directly to commit before review.

item.arguments: Use $fw:run after implementation is complete and review found no blocking issues, then finish the branch.
success_definition: Starts in review because code changed and implementation is done.
Uses a review-ready or commit-ready gate before finishing.
Emits a handoff card that carries verdict, findings, and evidence into commit.
Preserves spin as conditional rather than mandatory.
